metadata.name: EsoLang Output Prediction Dataset
metadata.description: A benchmark dataset where LLMs must predict the output of esoteric programming language code given specific inputs. Tests understanding of esoteric language semantics.

problems.title: Simple Character Output
problems.description: Given the following Brainfuck program that outputs characters, predict what will be printed to stdout. The program uses loops to build ASCII values and outputs them with '.' command.
problems.explanation: Cell 0 is set to 9*8=72 (ASCII 'H'), output. Then cell 0 gets 3*11=33 more to reach 105 (ASCII 'i'), output. Result: 'Hi'.

problems.title: Input Echo with Modification
problems.description: This Brainfuck program reads a character, increments its ASCII value by 1, and outputs it. Predict the output for the given input.
problems.explanation: The program reads 'A' (ASCII 65), increments to 66, outputs 'B'.

problems.title: Echo Input Characters
problems.description: This Brainfuck program reads characters from input and outputs them unchanged (cat program). Predict the output for the given input.
problems.explanation: The program reads a character, outputs it, and loops until input is exhausted. For input 'abc', it outputs 'abc'.

problems.title: Character Transformation
problems.description: This Brainfuck program reads a character and outputs the character that is 3 positions ahead in ASCII. Predict the output for input 'A'.
problems.explanation: Reads 'A' (ASCII 65), adds 3 to get 68, outputs 'D'. The + command increments the current cell value.

problems.title: Simple Arithmetic Output
problems.description: This Befunge-98 program performs arithmetic and outputs the result. Predict the output.
problems.explanation: Pushes 3 and 5, multiplies to get 15, pushes 2, adds to get 17, outputs as integer.

problems.title: Conditional Direction
problems.description: This Befunge-98 program uses the greater-than operator and conditional branching. The ` operator compares two values. Predict the output.
problems.explanation: Pushes 5 and 2, compares with ` (5>2=1), negates with ! (0), horizontal-if branches right to output 'Yes'.

problems.title: Direct Sum Calculation
problems.description: This Befunge-98 program pushes multiple numbers onto the stack and adds them together. Predict the output.
problems.explanation: Pushes 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 onto stack, then adds: 4+5=9, 3+9=12, 2+12=14, 1+14=15. Outputs 15.

problems.title: String Reversal Output
problems.description: This Befunge-98 program pushes characters, reverses them using the stack, and outputs. Predict the output.
problems.explanation: Pushes 'dcba' onto stack (reversed), outputs 4 characters which come out in LIFO order as 'abcd'.

problems.title: Push and Print Number
problems.description: This Whitespace program pushes a number onto the stack and prints it. The code uses [S]=space, [T]=tab, [N]=newline. Predict the output.
problems.explanation: Pushes binary 1010 (decimal 10) to stack, prints as integer.

problems.title: Simple Arithmetic
problems.description: This Whitespace program pushes two numbers, adds them, and prints the result. Predict the output.
problems.code_readable: Push 3, Push 5, Add, Print as number, End
problems.explanation: Pushes 3 and 5 to stack, adds them (3+5=8), prints result.

problems.title: Multiplication Result
problems.description: This Whitespace program multiplies two numbers and outputs the result. Predict the output.
problems.code_readable: Push 4, Push 3, Multiply, Print as number, End
problems.explanation: Pushes 4 and 3, multiplies (4*3=12), prints 12.

problems.title: Character Output
problems.description: This Whitespace program pushes ASCII values and prints them as characters. Predict the output.
problems.code_readable: Push 72 (H), Print as char, Push 105 (i), Print as char, End
problems.explanation: Pushes ASCII 72 ('H'), prints as char, pushes 105 ('i'), prints as char.

problems.title: Print Single Character
problems.description: This Unlambda program prints a single character. The .x combinator prints character x and returns its argument. Predict the output.
problems.explanation: Applies .! (print '!') to i (identity), printing '!' and returning i.

problems.title: Multiple Character Output
problems.description: This Unlambda program prints multiple characters using nested applications. Predict the output.
problems.explanation: Nested applications print 'H', 'i', '!' in sequence.

problems.title: K Combinator Usage
problems.description: This Unlambda program uses the K combinator (which returns its first argument and ignores the second). Predict the output.
problems.explanation: K takes .Y and .N, returns .Y (ignoring .N), then applies to i, printing 'Y'.

problems.title: S Combinator Application
problems.description: This Unlambda program demonstrates the S combinator. S x y z = x z (y z). Predict the output.
problems.explanation: S applied: (k .A) i ((k .B) i) = .A (constant), prints 'A'.

problems.title: Simple Numeric Output
problems.description: This Shakespeare program sets a character's value using noun phrases and outputs it as a number. The noun 'hero' has value 10. Predict the output.
problems.code:
The Test.

Romeo, a holder.
Juliet, a speaker.

Act I: The Act.
Scene I: The Scene.

Enter Romeo and Juliet.

Juliet: You are a hero. Open your heart.

Exeunt.
problems.explanation: Juliet tells Romeo 'You are a hero'. In SPL, 'hero' has value 10. 'Open your heart' outputs the value as a number.

problems.title: Arithmetic Sum
problems.description: This Shakespeare program computes the sum of two noun values and outputs the result. In SPL, 'cat' has value 3 and 'dog' has value 4. Predict the output.
problems.code:
The Sum.

Romeo, a holder.
Juliet, a speaker.

Act I: Add.
Scene I: Calculate.

Enter Romeo and Juliet.

Juliet: You are the sum of a cat and a dog. Open your heart.

Exeunt.
problems.explanation: 'The sum of a cat and a dog' computes cat(3) + dog(4) = 7. 'Open your heart' outputs this as a number.

problems.title: Product Calculation
problems.description: This Shakespeare program multiplies two noun values and outputs the result. In SPL, 'sun' has value 5 and 'cat' has value 3. Predict the output.
problems.code:
The Product.

Romeo, a holder.
Juliet, a speaker.

Act I: Multiply.
Scene I: Calculate.

Enter Romeo and Juliet.

Juliet: You are the product of a sun and a cat. Open your heart.

Exeunt.
problems.explanation: 'The product of a sun and a cat' computes sun(5) * cat(3) = 15. Output as number.

problems.title: Difference Calculation
problems.description: This Shakespeare program computes the difference between two noun values. In SPL, 'king' has value 20 and 'soldier' has value 8. Predict the output.
problems.code:
The Difference.

Romeo, a holder.
Juliet, a speaker.

Act I: Subtract.
Scene I: Calculate.

Enter Romeo and Juliet.

Juliet: You are the difference between a king and a soldier. Open your heart.

Exeunt.
problems.explanation: 'The difference between a king and a soldier' computes king(20) - soldier(8) = 12. Output as number.
